metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5531
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4,S3: AMPAR pulldowns (anti-GRIA1-4) yield Brorin (VWC2). In Noelin1-3(OLFM1-3) knockout mice, this interaction is lost.

Figure S11: in Brorin knockout mice, pulldowns against AMPAR yield reduced Noelin1 and Noelin2 levels (and no Brorin).

"Strikingly, the number of AMPARs detected by immuno-EM in the surface membrane was significantly reduced in both synapses and dendrites of Brorin-ablated neurons (Figure 5A), albeit to a lesser extent than by the removal of either Noe1-3 or Noe1 (Figure 2C), while the concomitant increase in AMPAR-free (silent) synapses was again about 2-fold (Figure 5A).

Similar to the Noelin KOs, the sEPSC amplitudes were decreased by the Brorin KO again with a stronger impact on INs than on principle cells as observed before (Figures 3A and 5B); the mEPSC amplitude was reduced by 23% compared with WT (Figure 5B).

Finally, Brorin-depleted CA1 PCs failed to effectively and robustly recruit additional AMPARs into the synapse in response to stimulation by the pairing-protocol (Figure 5C) although the observed impairment relative to WT was less pronounced than induced by removal of the Noelins (Figure 3).". Experimental description: Figure S2c; "Brorin KO mouse (MMRRC-UC-Davis). The first coding exon (exon2) was eliminated using a targeting vector"

Vwc2 (Brorin, NM_177033.3)
